title: Tabs orientation is used for styling but not passed to Base UI root
reasoning: The component accepts `orientation` from `TabsPrimitive.Root.Props` and writes it to a custom `data-orientation` attribute, but because `orientation` is destructured out of `props`, it is never forwarded to `TabsPrimitive.Root`. Callers using `orientation="vertical"` will get vertical-looking styling while the underlying Base UI tabs root still receives its default orientation, which can break expected keyboard navigation and accessibility semantics.
reproduction: Render `<Tabs orientation="vertical">` with multiple triggers, then inspect the underlying Base UI root props/behavior: the primitive is not given `orientation="vertical"` even though the wrapper applies `data-orientation="vertical"`.
recommendation: Pass `orientation={orientation}` to `TabsPrimitive.Root` in addition to the styling data attribute.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are included for this feature, and the package manifest exposes no test command. A visual-only snapshot would also miss this unless it checks primitive props or keyboard behavior.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a component test that renders `Tabs` with `orientation="vertical"` and verifies vertical keyboard behavior or that the primitive receives the vertical orientation attribute/semantics expected by Base UI.
minimumFixScope: Update `frontend/src/components/ui/tabs.tsx` so `TabsPrimitive.Root` receives the destructured `orientation` prop.
